stories.headline: NBI witness links Rodrigo Duterte's ICC case to threat assessment in VP impeachment trial
stories.summary: On day five of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, NBI official Jeremy Lotoc testified investigators weighed former President Rodrigo Duterte's ICC case in assessing the vice president's alleged capability to threaten President Marcos, First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez. Under cross-examination, Lotoc said he had no personal knowledge of an alleged hitman plot but maintained the NBI's findings on grave threats and inciting to sedition. The court is set to rule Wednesday on the prosecution's bid to subpoena Duterte's bank and tax records.
stories.context: The threats allegedly occurred during a November 2024 online press briefing; the trial concerns whether Duterte should be removed from office over the allegations.
stories.watch_for: The impeachment court is due to rule Wednesday on the subpoena request, and NBI Director Melvin Matibag's testimony has been rescheduled to July 20.

stories.headline: UP-PGH doctors declare detained senator fit to appear before anti-graft court
stories.summary: Independent doctors from the University of the Philippines-Philippine General Hospital concluded that Senator Rodante Marcoleta, in police hospital custody since his July 6 arrest on plunder charges, is medically fit to be presented to the Sandiganbayan. Police said it is now up to the anti-graft court to decide whether Marcoleta can be transferred from hospital detention to a regular jail facility.
stories.context: Marcoleta was arrested over an alleged 75-million-peso campaign-fund plunder case and has remained under medical observation at a police hospital for reported back pain, degenerative disc disease and pneumonia.
stories.watch_for: The Sandiganbayan is due to decide on his transfer to jail detention.

stories.headline: Philippine stocks slip further as Middle East tensions weigh on markets
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index fell 0.15%, or 9.70 points, to close at 6,256.02 on Tuesday, while the broader All Shares index dropped 0.09% to 3,379.94, as investors grew more cautious over escalating US-Iran hostilities and rising global oil prices. Trading volume was thin, with many investors staying on the sidelines, BusinessWorld reported.

stories.headline: Marcos inaugurates first phase of solar-battery project developers call world's largest
stories.summary: President Marcos inaugurated Phase 1 of the Meralco-backed MTerra Solar project in Gapan, Nueva Ecija, which developers describe as the world's largest integrated solar and battery storage facility, with 1,373 megawatts of solar capacity and 825 megawatts of battery storage now energized. Marcos said the project reflects the government's push to diversify the country's energy sources. The full project, spanning five towns in Nueva Ecija and Bulacan, is designed to reach 3,500 megawatts of solar capacity and 4,500 megawatt-hours of battery storage.
stories.context: Terra Solar Philippines Inc. and Meralco PowerGen Corp. developed the project; once complete, it is expected to supply electricity to about 2.4 million households and cut annual carbon emissions by roughly 4.3 million metric tons.
stories.watch_for: Phase 1 is about 91% complete, with full commercial operation targeted for August 2026, while Phase 2 is expected to be completed in 2027.

stories.headline: Magnitude 5.2 quake jolts off Sarangani Island, felt across South Cotabato and Davao
stories.summary: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake struck nine kilometers east of Sarangani Island in Davao Occidental at 9:15 p.m. Tuesday, at a depth of 10 kilometers, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. The strongest shaking, Intensity III, was recorded in Malungon, Sarangani, with lighter intensity felt in General Santos City, Tupi, Kidapawan City, Digos City and Santa Maria. No damage or injuries were immediately reported.
stories.context: Phivolcs said the quake was an aftershock of the magnitude 7.8 earthquake off Maasim, Sarangani on June 8 and the magnitude 6.5 quake off Balut Island on June 26.

stories.headline: Tropical Storm Josie weakens to low-pressure area ahead of exiting Philippine waters
stories.summary: Tropical Storm Josie entered the Philippine Area of Responsibility around 12:30 a.m. Tuesday, weakened to a tropical depression by 8 a.m. and further degraded into a low-pressure area by 2 p.m., PAGASA said. A PAGASA weather specialist attributed the weakening to high vertical wind shear and said the system was expected to exit the country's area of responsibility by Tuesday evening or early Wednesday and could dissipate within 24 hours.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said the weakened system is less likely to directly affect Philippine weather in the coming days as it exits the area of responsibility.

stories.headline: Monsoon rains and high tides force class suspensions across Luzon provinces
stories.summary: Classes were suspended in parts of Bulacan, Pampanga, Pangasinan and Isabela on Tuesday due to flooding and high tides linked to the southwest monsoon, as well as localized power outages, according to local government announcements compiled by GMA News. In Macabebe and Masantol, Pampanga, authorities cited flooding from monsoon rains combined with a 5.2-foot high tide and extended the suspension through July 16.

stories.headline: Military tightens security in Basilan ahead of BARMM gun ban and September elections
stories.summary: The Armed Forces' Western Mindanao Command intensified security operations against remaining armed groups in Basilan and nearby areas ahead of a region-wide gun ban taking effect Thursday, officials said, as the Bangsamoro region prepares for its first parliamentary elections on September 14. Security officials said remnants of the Abu Sayyaf Group and splinter factions of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters could be exploited by local political actors during the election period.
stories.context: The September 14 parliamentary polls mark a major test of BARMM's transition from decades of armed conflict toward democratic governance under the Bangsamoro Organic Law.
stories.watch_for: A region-wide gun ban takes effect Thursday, July 16, ahead of the September elections.

stories.headline: Government says OFW repatriation from Middle East continues, though pace slows
stories.summary: The Department of Migrant Workers said it has repatriated 10,580 overseas Filipino workers and their dependents from the Middle East so far amid the US-Israel conflict with Iran, including 50 people from Kuwait who arrived this week. DMW Undersecretary Felicitas Bay said repatriation requests have declined compared with the April-to-May peak but assistance remains available to those who need it.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran for third consecutive night as Iran hits tankers, Gulf bases
stories.summary: US Central Command carried out a third consecutive night of strikes on Iranian military targets, including sites in Bushehr, Chah Bahar, Jask, Konarak, Abu Musa and Bandar Abbas, according to Al Jazeera. Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps responded with strikes on two UAE-flagged oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, killing one Indian crew member and wounding eight others, along with attacks on US military facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait and a Jordanian air base. Oil prices surged more than 9%, with Brent crude nearing $85 a barrel, and shipping traffic through the strait dropped sharply.
stories.context: The escalation follows the collapse of a mid-June US-Iran ceasefire understanding; President Trump said the US had reinstated a blockade of Iran's southern ports.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon resume Rome talks on troop withdrawal from south Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanese and Israeli delegations met at the US embassy in Rome for a sixth round of talks aimed at implementing a US-brokered framework agreement to end their conflict, focused on a phased Israeli troop withdrawal from two areas of southern Lebanon. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun instructed his delegation to demand an immediate start to the Israeli pullout before further discussion, while Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar said Israel was ready to move forward with the withdrawal plan.
stories.context: Israel and Hezbollah reached the framework agreement in Washington on June 26, following renewed fighting that began March 2; the renewed US-Iran hostilities in the Gulf have raised concern the agreement could unravel.
stories.watch_for: The talks were scheduled to continue over two days, July 14-15.

stories.headline: Houthis strike Saudi airport with missiles and drones, declare 2022 truce over
stories.summary: Yemen's Houthi movement said it fired ballistic missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia's Abha International Airport, which the Saudi-led coalition said it intercepted, in retaliation for a strike a day earlier on Sanaa International Airport meant to block a plane carrying a Houthi delegation from Iran. A Houthi spokesperson said the group considers the informal de-escalation in place since 2022 to be over and warned it could disrupt shipping through the Bab al-Mandeb strait if the blockade on Sanaa's airport continues. No casualties were reported from either strike.
